title: Planning State V1 Schema
goal: Define a compact planning-state/v1 contract that makes work maturity explicit without migrating the current state file in this slice.
non_goals: Do not migrate .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml to the new shape in this slice.
Do not implement all maturity-aware summary behavior in this slice.
Do not build a full project-management schema.

intent.outcome: Planning state has a schema-backed maturity vocabulary and validation coverage for ready and active items.
intent.constraints: Keep the contract repo-, tracker-, agent-, and language-agnostic; preserve current state compatibility until #500.
intent.latitude: Choose JSON Schema or typed validation as the smallest durable contract, and add docs only when they reduce future guesswork.
intent.escalation: Stop before rewriting summary/reconcile around the new shape or migrating current state.

execution.milestone: Define planning-state/v1 and validate representative maturity states.
execution.next_step: Archive this slice and continue the planning-state-maturity lane through #498-#501 when selected.
execution.proof: planning package tests, ruff, planning doctor, planning summary, and planning-surface checks

invariants: New maturity states do not become a second active-planning authority until migration is selected.
Ready slices require enough handoff detail to prevent trial-and-error implementation.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Planning state should model broad direction, lanes, ready slices, active execplans, review, closeout, and durable residue explicitly for multi-agent handoff.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After #497 lands, continue with #498/#499/#500/#501 as selected by summary/reconcile.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: A schema vocabulary for maturity-based planning state.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: State migration, summary rewrite, role metadata, and closeout transition helpers.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Roadmap promotion still needs a package helper; #526/#528 already own that correct-by-design gap.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: planning-state/v1 metadata, allowed maturity values, and ready/active/closed validation are implemented for opted-in state files.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Future slices must validate role metadata, maturity-aware summary output, migration, and closeout transitions.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #498 or #499 depending on whether role metadata or summary query behavior blocks migration.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Repeat until done; roadmap selected #497 as the next planning-state-maturity slice.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Make planning state less vague by defining explicit maturity semantics before migration.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Define planning-state/v1 schema/validator coverage without migrating current state.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check that this slice defines semantics and validation, not just prose.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: planning package contract/schema/parser/tests, planning bootstrap state template if needed, active planning files
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q; cd packages/planning; uv run ruff check .; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any migration of current state shape or broad summary/reconcile rewrite.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: generated CLI migration, agent aid lifecycle, full structured-file inventory

stop_conditions.stop when: planning-state/v1 semantics and representative validation are landed and #497 can be closed honestly.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The next needed change is migration or multi-command authoring scaffolds rather than schema definition.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Work starts implementing #498-#501 in the same slice.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Existing state compatibility breaks or summary cannot read current state.

context_budget.live working set: issue #497, current state parser/writer, planning tests, this execplan
context_budget.recoverable later: full roadmap lane #496, historical archived plans, unrelated open issues
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, intent_satisfaction, closure_check, and closeout_distillation.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace skills --target . --task "planning-state v1 schema" --format json
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: not needed unless parser context proves unclear
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Continue #497 from planning-state-v1-schema.plan.json and focused planning package tests.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: subagent findings contradict this scope, tests reveal migration is inseparable, or contract tooling already owns this schema.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make planning state explicit about maturity states.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not migrate current state yet; keep host/provider/language assumptions out of the schema.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact schema file location and whether validation is JSON Schema, typed validator, or both.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: Schema choices force broad state migration or compatibility policy decisions.

references.label: Define planning-state v1 schema with explicit maturity states

immediate_next_action: Archive this completed #497 slice and continue #498-#501 when selected.
invariants: Ready and active maturity states have concrete required fields.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Maturity values: idea, candidate, shaped, ready, active, closed.
Active items point to execplans instead of duplicating implementation detail.
open_questions_to_close: Slices may be represented as first-class work_items in the v1 contract while existing todo/roadmap buckets remain compatibility views until #500.
validation_commands: cd packages/planning; uv run ruff check .
completion_criteria: planning-state/v1 allowed maturity values and required fields are defined.
Ready items require next_action, done_when, proof, refs or owner, and review role.
Active items require an execplan reference.
Tests cover valid and invalid maturity records.

execution_run.what happened: Added planning-state/v1 constants, opt-in state validation warnings, top-level metadata preservation in the state serializer, and focused tests for valid and invalid maturity records.
execution_run.scope touched: planning package state validation and tests plus active planning state
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "planning_state_v1 or compact_toml or planning_cli_dogfoods"; cd packages/planning; uv run pytest tests/test_installer.py; cd packages/planning; uv run ruff check .; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json
execution_run.result for continuation: The schema contract slice is complete; planning-state-maturity remains open for role metadata, summary behavior, migration, and closeout transitions.

finished_run_review.config compliance: used summary/report/reconcile/skills and promoted checked-in planning before implementation
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: medium; schema definition must not become migration

proof_report.validation proof: cd packages/planning; uv run pytest tests/test_installer.py; cd packages/planning; uv run ruff check .; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json
proof_report.proof achieved now: planning-state/v1 validation accepts valid active/ready/shaped records and warns on invalid maturity, ready, active, and closed records while preserving legacy compatibility.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: 144 planning package tests passed, ruff passed, planning doctor reported healthy, and live summary showed planning-backed state with zero warnings.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Define planning-state v1 schema with explicit maturity states.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: planning-state/v1 kind/schema_version, maturity/status vocabulary, ready/active/closed validation, and tests are implemented.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make package use cheaper by making current state queryable and explicit instead of inferred from vague buckets.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Define compact machine-readable semantics before broader migration.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can agents tell whether work is idea, shaped, ready, active, or closed without interpreting bucket names?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #497 is satisfied, but parent #496 still includes #498-#501 for roles, query behavior, migration, and closeout integration.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: planning package tests, ruff, planning doctor, summary, and planning-surface checks
closure_check.reopen trigger: planning state still requires inference from todo/roadmap buckets after #497

durable_residue.learned constraint: Maturity semantics belong in planning contracts before migration rewrites current state.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Agents should not infer work maturity from vague buckets.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Defined planning-state/v1 as an opt-in checked-in state contract with explicit maturity/status values and required-field validation for ready, active, and closed records.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: 144 planning tests passed, planning ruff passed, planning doctor healthy, live summary warning_count 0.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Contract behavior is encoded in installer validation tests; no Memory note needed.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): planning state contract in package code/tests and current state metadata

signals found: Roadmap-to-active promotion still required manual state and execplan construction.
signals routed: #526 and #528 cover scaffold/helper routing for schema-backed artifacts and validation friction.

drift_log: 2026-04-28: Promoted #497 from roadmap-needs-promotion state into an active execplan.
2026-04-28: Implemented planning-state/v1 validation as an opt-in contract without migrating current state shape.
